final-neighborhood-resource-guide-histor.label: Final Capstone: Neighborhood Resource Guide History
final-neighborhood-resource-guide-histor.summary: Build a complete local history for a neighborhood resource guide while keeping changes focused, generated artifacts ignored, and the final repository easy to hand off.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Rename the Guide and Remove the Obsolete Note
Commit and Verify the Final Handoff
Create the Guide Baseline
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt:
Start the final capstone project. In step 1 of 6, Turn the existing `resource-guide/` folder into a Git repository on `main`, configure the repository-only learner identity, stage the initial guide files, and commit `Start neighborhood resource guide`.

First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter. Then enter `resource-guide/`, type each Git command, and press Enter after every command.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.starterCode:
# First type: bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
# Press Enter, then follow the capstone step in the prompt.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
cd resource-guide
git init -b main
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git add README.md pages/contact.md pages/guide-draft.md notes/old-phone-tree.txt
git commit -m "Start neighborhood resource guide"
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions:
Turn the existing `resource-guide/` folder into a Git repository on `main`, configure the repository-only learner identity, stage the initial guide files, and commit `Start neighborhood resource guide`.

First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter. Then enter `resource-guide/`, type each Git command, and press Enter after every command.

main_sh.content:
# First type: bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
# Press Enter, then follow the capstone step in the prompt.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- "resource-guide"
mkdir -p -- "resource-guide/pages" "resource-guide/notes" "resource-guide/generated"
printf '%s\n' '# Neighborhood Resource Guide' '' 'Welcome to the neighborhood resource guide.' > "resource-guide/README.md"
printf '%s\n' '# Contact Information' '' 'Help Desk Hours: 9 AM - 4 PM, Monday to Friday' > "resource-guide/pages/contact.md"
printf '%s\n' '# Guide Draft' '' 'Use this page to find neighborhood help.' > "resource-guide/pages/guide-draft.md"
printf '%s\n' 'Legacy volunteer phone tree' > "resource-guide/notes/old-phone-tree.txt"

main_sh.content:
bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
cd resource-guide
git init -b main
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git add README.md pages/contact.md pages/guide-draft.md notes/old-phone-tree.txt
git commit -m "Start neighborhood resource guide"

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- "resource-guide"
mkdir -p -- "resource-guide/pages" "resource-guide/notes" "resource-guide/generated"
printf '%s\n' '# Neighborhood Resource Guide' '' 'Welcome to the neighborhood resource guide.' > "resource-guide/README.md"
printf '%s\n' '# Contact Information' '' 'Help Desk Hours: 9 AM - 4 PM, Monday to Friday' > "resource-guide/pages/contact.md"
printf '%s\n' '# Guide Draft' '' 'Use this page to find neighborhood help.' > "resource-guide/pages/guide-draft.md"
printf '%s\n' 'Legacy volunteer phone tree' > "resource-guide/notes/old-phone-tree.txt"
git -C "resource-guide" init -q -b main
git -C "resource-guide" config user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C "resource-guide" config user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git -C "resource-guide" add README.md pages/contact.md pages/guide-draft.md notes/old-phone-tree.txt
git -C "resource-guide" commit -q -m "Start neighborhood resource guide"

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Rename the Guide and Remove the Obsolete Note
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt:
Continue the final capstone project from the previous working step. In step 5 of 6, Use Git-aware file operations to rename `pages/guide-draft.md` to `pages/getting-help.md` and remove `notes/old-phone-tree.txt`. Keep these staged changes together with the staged `.gitignore`; do not commit yet.

First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter to restore the staged ignore rule. Enter `resource-guide/`, type `git mv` and press Enter, then type `git rm` and press Enter.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- "resource-guide"
mkdir -p -- "resource-guide/pages" "resource-guide/notes" "resource-guide/generated"
printf '%s\n' '# Neighborhood Resource Guide' '' 'Welcome to the neighborhood resource guide.' > "resource-guide/README.md"
printf '%s\n' '# Contact Information' '' 'Help Desk Hours: 9 AM - 4 PM, Monday to Friday' > "resource-guide/pages/contact.md"
printf '%s\n' '# Guide Draft' '' 'Use this page to find neighborhood help.' > "resource-guide/pages/guide-draft.md"
printf '%s\n' 'Legacy volunteer phone tree' > "resource-guide/notes/old-phone-tree.txt"
git -C "resource-guide" init -q -b main
git -C "resource-guide" config user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C "resource-guide" config user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git -C "resource-guide" add README.md pages/contact.md pages/guide-draft.md notes/old-phone-tree.txt
git -C "resource-guide" commit -q -m "Start neighborhood resource guide"
printf '%s\n' '# Local Services' '' '- Community pantry' '- Senior transport' > "resource-guide/pages/services.md"
git -C "resource-guide" add pages/services.md
git -C "resource-guide" commit -q -m "Add local services page"
printf '%s\n' '# Contact Information' '' 'Help Desk Hours: 9 AM - 5 PM, Monday to Friday' > "resource-guide/pages/contact.md"
git -C "resource-guide" add pages/contact.md
git -C "resource-guide" commit -q -m "Update help desk hours"
printf '%s\n' '<html>generated preview</html>' > "resource-guide/generated/preview.html"

intro.title: Welcome to the Final Capstone
intro.bodyMarkdown:
In this final capstone, you will apply your Git skills to create a local history for a neighborhood resource guide. Your task is to ensure that the guide is well-maintained, with clear commit history and ignored generated artifacts. This project will help future volunteers understand changes, recover from mistakes, and trust the final handoff.

### Project Brief



**Scenario:** A neighborhood help desk is replacing a folder of loose text files with a maintained resource guide. Future volunteers must be able to see when service information changed, recover from small mistakes, and trust the final handoff.

**Your role:** Junior developer preparing the neighborhood resource guide for long-term maintenance

**Workspace:** One cumulative terminal workspace rooted at resource-guide/ with pages/, notes/, generated/, README.md, and no starting repository

**Deliverable:** One six-step local Git capstone with four meaningful commits, ignored generated previews, a tracked file rename, a safe obsolete-file removal, required final content, and a clean handoff state.
